context: Current closing price of Carrier Global (CARR): $59.71 USD as of 2026-04-16.
justification_text:
The point estimates reflect the expected value of whatever metric we are forecasting, at different time horizons. In the shortest horizon (1 day), the point estimate is based primarily on recent data, because there’s limited time for significant changes to occur. That’s why the value for "1d" (59.8) is so close to the current or prevailing trend. Moving to 1 week, the point estimate shifts slightly higher to 60.1, reflecting a broader range of influences and potential developments over the medium term. By 1 month, the estimate rises further to 61.5, as there is more room for gradual directional change to build up over time.

The width of the confidence intervals increases as the forecasting horizon lengthens, mainly because uncertainty naturally grows the further out we predict. At 1 day, the uncertainty is minimal since most underlying factors tend to stay constant over short periods. But over a week or a month, more variables could come into play—for instance, changes in market conditions, external shocks, or shifts in behavior. This is why the 90% confidence interval broadens substantially from a tight range of 58.9 to 60.7 at 1 day, to a much wider range of 55.5 to 67.5 by 1 month.

Certain risks or events could push the actual outcome outside of the stated confidence intervals. At shorter horizons, these risks might include unexpected disruptions such as breaking news, sudden market volatility, or one-off events with immediate impact. Over longer horizons, larger structural changes could play a role—such as economic data releases, policy changes, or geopolitical developments. These events are inherently harder to predict or quantify, which is why they remain outside the modeled estimates. While we aim to capture 90% of plausible outcomes within the confidence intervals, extreme or highly atypical scenarios could fall into the remaining 10%.
